summary: Full backend + frontend pass for Barcode Quest. 24/24 backend pytest cases pass (auth, character, scan, inventory, equip/use/destroy, upgrade rejection AND success, market price-band/list/buy/cancel + guardrails). Frontend tested on mobile viewport: redirect-to-login, register, login, character screen, scan (manual fallback), inventory with filter chips, item detail modal with EQUIP/USE/DESTROY/UPGRADE/LIST actions, market browse + my-listings tabs. All flows functional end-to-end.

integration_issues.issue: Reveal Modal sometimes not detectable via Playwright wait_for_selector even after item is created (item appears in inventory). Likely React Native Modal portal/timing on web. Functionally correct - items persist and visible in inventory.

issues: Many `shadow*` and `textShadow*` deprecated style warnings in console - should migrate to `boxShadow`/`textShadow` per RN web newer API (non-blocking)
props.pointerEvents deprecation warning on overlays - migrate to style.pointerEvents

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add `testID` to tab buttons in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx (e.g. via tabBarButtonTestID) so navigation tests can click tabs directly. Currently tests must use route URLs (/character, /scan, /inventory, /market) which works fine.
OPTIONAL: Address deprecated `shadow*` / `textShadow*` style warnings by migrating to `boxShadow` / `textShadow` per RN-web 0.79+. Non-blocking.

OPTIONAL: The testID convention is inconsistent: inventory items use `item-<id>` (good) and action buttons use `action-equip` (no `item-` prefix). The original request mentioned `inv-item-` prefix which doesn't exist. Document the actual testIDs in PRD for future test agents.
Review: scan-reveal Modal may render asynchronously on web; consider using `useEffect` log or alternate visibility signal if E2E reveal-modal assertion becomes critical.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 611 lines - approaching size limit; consider splitting into routes/auth.py, routes/market.py, routes/items.py for future maintainability (not blocking).
`grant_xp` while-loop is fine but no cap on max_level - a barrage of scans/equips could theoretically pump levels indefinitely. Acceptable for MVP.

Market buy flow is NOT atomic - two separate Mongo updates for gold transfer + item ownership. If a process crash occurs between updates, inventory/gold could desync. Consider Mongo transactions or compensating logic for production.
`use_item` heals even if HP is already at max - silently no-op. Could return informative response (still status 200 is acceptable).

success_rate.backend: 100% (24/24, 1 skipped due to test-data conditional path)
success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows verified; only scan-reveal modal selector flaked - functionality works)

seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_ prefixed users created via /api/auth/register during test runs. Seeded user test@bq.com retains items in inventory.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Inventory items use testID `item-<uuid>` (not `inv-item-`). Filter chips: `filter-<label>` (label is uppercase: ALL/WEAPON/ARMOR/ACCESS/CONSUM/UPGRADE). Item detail modal: testID `item-detail-modal`; close button `item-detail-close`; action buttons: `action-equip`, `action-unequip`, `action-use`, `action-destroy`, `action-list`, `action-upgrade-toggle`. Scan: manual fallback button `scan-manual-open-fallback` (perm screen) OR `scan-manual-open` (camera screen). Reveal: `scan-reveal`, `scan-reveal-name`, `scan-reveal-dismiss`. Tab nav has NO testIDs - navigate by URL (/character, /scan, /inventory, /market). Use BASE_URL from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (https://barcode-armor-craft.preview.emergentagent.com). AI scan takes 2-5s; allow >25s wait. User levels up via XP grants from scan/equip/upgrade - tests that depend on level 1 must use a fresh user.
rca_of_issue: No production bugs found. Initial test failure (upgrade rejection) was a test-side issue: the test user had gained XP from prior scans and leveled up, so the upgrade endpoint correctly allowed upgrade. Fixed by detecting current user level and finding an item AT user level before asserting 400.
